trim_reasons: PHI is a +14.5 underdog — a large deficit game script means Maxey may face extended garbage-time or lineup changes in Q4 if the game is out of hand early, even with proj_min=40. The blowout risk on the road underdog side suppresses the floor more than the spread adjustment alone captures.

Volatile AST with no iron_floor, min confidence (74%), in a large-spread game where PHI trails by double digits — per historical miss pattern, AST T6+ in CLOSE or blowout scenarios against tough opponents at minimum confidence with no iron_floor is structurally borderline. The PHI blowout underdog script compresses assist opportunities as Maxey shifts to hero-ball isolation scoring, exactly the mechanism that caused the Edwards AST miss.

SAS is an 11.5-point favorite with BLOWOUT_RISK — a blowout win triggers Q4 rest for rotation starters including Vassell, and his min_floor of 26.7 (vs avg 30.6) creates a real minutes-compression scenario in a runaway game. Even after the blowout penalty, the structural rest risk for a secondary perimeter player in a lopsided game remains.

Min_floor of 22.8 against an avg of 29.5 is a wide range for a BLOWOUT_RISK game — if SAS blows out POR early, Fox's minutes could land at the low end of his distribution, and T10 at the floor requires he stay active into the fourth. The spread-adjusted confidence already prices this in, but the structural minutes fragility in a blowout scenario is real given the min_floor gap.

Volatile 3PM shooter with a min_floor of 22.8 in a blowout game — Fox's 3PM relies on him playing full minutes and taking perimeter shots, both of which compress in a lopsided SAS win. The H2H sample is only n=4, and the BLOWOUT game script could eliminate Fox's late-game shot volume entirely.

VOLATILE scorer with a min_floor of only 25.0 (avg 28.5) in a -11.5 BLOWOUT_RISK game — Wembanyama is the most likely SAS star to be rested in Q4 of a blowout, and his post_miss_hr of only 71% (lift=0.80) is the weakest bounce-back signal on the slate, meaning this tier is not buttressed by a strong recovery pattern.

VOLATILE secondary scorer in a -11.5 BLOWOUT_RISK game with FG_MARGIN_NEG already applied — Castle is precisely the BLOWOUT_SECONDARY_SCORER archetype the historical miss pattern flagged, and his min_floor of 25.7 vs avg 31.2 means a blowout-shortened appearance (normal variance in a runaway game) puts T10 at genuine risk. Per audit history, step-downs for BLOWOUT_SECONDARY_SCORER with FG_MARGIN_NEG are necessary but may still be insufficient when the game script is extreme.

VOLATILE with FG_MARGIN_NEG:-9% and iron_floor at T20 in a -14.5 BLOWOUT_RISK game — Brown is a secondary scorer who may see reduced Q4 usage if BOS cruises, and his iron_floor classification only protects the floor tier structurally, not against blowout-driven minutes compression. The typical_miss of 10 pts below floor signals wide downside distribution.

Pritchard is explicitly a BLOWOUT_SECONDARY_SCORER in a -14.5 game — his min_floor of 23.4 means a single early-benching in a runaway BOS win puts T10 at risk, and his trend=down signals recent scoring compression. The blowout penalty was applied but secondary scorers in lopsided games face structural fourth-quarter irrelevance.

FG_COLD:-21% triggered a mandatory step-down and the def_recency flag='tough' shows LAL has tightened dramatically (L5 avg 100.8 vs L15 avg 111.9, -9.9%). Sengun's post_miss_hr of 74% with lift=0.99 is essentially neutral — no reliable bounce-back signal supports recovery from cold-shooting form against a defense that has hardened over the past five games.

VOLATILE 3PM shooter with post_miss_hr=70% (lift=0.80) — the weakest bounce-back signal on a volatile prop, and the def_recency flag='tough' confirms LAL has been a hardened defense over the last 5 games. Raw avg of 2.6 means T2 is close to the mean, and VOLATILE distribution makes a 0-1 3PM outcome structurally plausible against a tightened defense.
